item.title: AstraZeneca EMERALD-2 Trial
item.description:
• Summary: AstraZeneca is advancing the EMERALD-2 trial, which evaluates enzalutamide, an androgen receptor inhibitor, in metastatic castration-resistant prostate cancer. The trial is part of AstraZeneca's broader oncology portfolio, with data expected to inform treatment strategies and regulatory submissions.
  • MoAs: Enzalutamide is an androgen receptor antagonist that blocks androgen signaling, inhibiting tumor growth in prostate cancer.
  • Sources: ApexOnco

item.title: Radiotherapeutics & Cancer Isotopes
item.description:
• Summary: The growing role of radiotherapeutics and isotopes in cancer treatment is highlighted, showcasing how these agents are changing oncology care by targeting tumors with precision. This approach leverages radioactive isotopes to deliver localized radiation, improving efficacy and reducing side effects.
  • MoAs: Radiotherapeutics involve the use of radioactive isotopes (e.g., alpha or beta emitters) that accumulate in tumor tissues via targeting mechanisms, emitting radiation to destroy cancer cells while sparing healthy tissue.
  • Sources: BioSpace
